PROMPT: I'm doing a code quality audit of the `storyteller` repository at `/data/repos/storyteller`. Here's what I need you to do: First, look at all the branches in that repo and find the one that is NOT `main` (i.e., the feature/development branch). Then get the full git log for that branch (retrieve at least 30 commits) and identify the SHA of the OLDEST commit (i.e., the last one in the log). Next, get the full diff of that oldest commit to see what files it changed. From the diff, extract the exact names of every file that was added or modified (not deleted) in that commit. Then, for each of those files, look up the current version of the file in the `main` branch of the `storyteller` GitHub repository (owner: `srobinson`) and count how many lines of code each file currently has. Finally, write a summary report to `/data/storyteller_audit_report.txt` that lists: (1) the name of the non-main branch, (2) the SHA of the oldest commit on that branch, (3) for each file added/modified in that oldest commit, its path and current line count on main. The report must include the exact oldest commit SHA and exact current line counts.
